entities.gloss: a per-unit scalar tuning parameter governing the strength of an intervention that stochastically deactivates each unit
a directed connectivity structure specifying asymmetric pairwise links among units, assumed locally acyclic
a weighted operator formed by attenuating each directed link by the per-unit retention factor (one minus the tuning parameter)
the leading spectral value (largest eigenvalue) of the weighted operator, a scalar summarizing overall propagation strength
a critical value of the leading spectral value (unity) separating the connected regime from the fragmented regime
a collective aggregate measuring the fraction of units remaining in the giant mutually-reachable component; non-zero in the connected phase and vanishing past the critical point
a discrete categorical state an agent can adopt, one of many mutually competing alternatives
the count of agents currently holding a given categorical state
the aggregate distribution of state sizes across the whole population
the spatial separation between two agents or regions
a pairwise similarity measure between states reflecting shared lineage
a fixed spatial partition or barrier constraining which agents can interact
a composite state in which an agent simultaneously holds two categorical states at once
the competitive interaction through which agents adopt or abandon states, biased toward more populous ones
the probability per update step that an agent switches its categorical state
a time-evolving network of pairwise links among elementary units
elementary units whose activity and linking patterns change over time
a cohesive, densely-connected substructure that emerges within the network
the size (number of constituent units) of a substructure, which selects its dynamical regime
the rate at which a substructure's constituent membership turns over
the persistence duration (lifetime) of a substructure
the degree of sustained participation of a unit in a given substructure
a discrete tuning parameter fixing the number of admissible local states allowed per unit
a scalar tuning parameter measuring the mean density of pairwise constraints per unit
a large sparse random network of pairwise incompatibility constraints linking the units
the space of admissible joint configurations that satisfy every pairwise constraint
the entropy (log count) of admissible configurations, a collective aggregate over the whole system
the fraction of units pinned to a single admissible value across a dominant cluster; zero below a critical point and non-zero above it
a critical value of the constraint-density parameter at which the configuration space fragments into exponentially many disconnected clusters
a critical value at which statistical weight condenses onto a finite number of the largest clusters
a critical value at which a finite fraction of units becomes frozen within every dominant cluster
a critical value of the constraint-density parameter beyond which no admissible configuration exists at all
a substrate of discrete units joined by pairwise links over which influence can spread, with a heavy-tailed distribution of per-unit link counts
the local connectivity of a unit, i.e. its number of incident links
a cutoff on a unit's connectivity above which the unit is eligible to be selected for deactivation
the fraction of units that are deactivated, tuned upward to drive the substrate toward loss of global connectivity
the size of the largest mutually reachable cluster of active units, a collective aggregate that collapses toward zero past a critical point
a purely local selection procedure that picks a random unit and follows one of its links to a more-connected neighbor, using only neighborhood information rather than global structure
a structured web of pairwise connections among a large number of interacting units
the extreme values of the spectrum of the pairwise-connectivity operator, which govern how the connection structure shapes collective dynamics
the size of the system (number of units), driven toward a large-size limit
the across-realizations distribution of X2 over an ensemble of randomly generated structures
the degree of concentration (sharp peakedness) of X4, which becomes strongly peaked in the large-size limit
a collective dynamical process unfolding on the network whose behavior is determined by X2
an angular phase variable carried by each individual unit in a large coupled ensemble
the intrinsic advancement rate assigned to each unit that drives its own dynamics
the pairwise interaction strength that links the states of connected units
a collective aggregate measuring the degree of mutual coherence across the ensemble, becoming appreciable past a critical point
the network of connections over which units exchange influence
a critical value of the coupling above which collective coherence emerges
a two-mode distribution of intrinsic rates that partitions the units into two opposing classes with distinct roles
a topological substrate of nodes joined by pairwise links over which a process propagates
the count of direct links attached to a given node
a per-node status indicating whether the node currently holds the propagated quantity
a collective aggregate measuring the average maximal fraction of a node's neighbors that mutually participate in the shared process
the elapsed duration required for the process to reach that aggregate fraction
the likelihood that a given node becomes a target of the propagated process
a scalar aggregate measuring the fraction of pairwise couplings that are mutual/two-way across the whole system
a fixed input prescribing, for each unit, how many outgoing and incoming couplings it carries
a statistical dependency that biases which units couple to which, based on their connectivity magnitudes
the ensemble of directed pairwise couplings among the units of the system
an ordered hierarchy of successively higher-order contributions summing to the aggregate
the topology of pairwise connections linking many units into a single interacting system
the local state of an individual unit embedded in the system
the strength of interaction transmitted between units along a connection
a collective aggregate that emerges from the many units acting together
a global objective quantity that an optimization process seeks to extremize
a reconfigurable connectivity substrate linking interacting units, whose wiring can change over time
a per-unit internal attribute that evolves through local interactions along the substrate
a collective aggregate measuring the extent of global alignment among units, i.e. the emergence of aligned clusters
a scalar tuning parameter setting how strongly and quickly the substrate reconfigures itself
the feedback link that ties unit-state similarity to reconfiguration of the connectivity substrate
a critical point separating a globally-aligned regime from a fragmented, split regime
the temporal-evolution profile of the system's aggregate state, at both early and late times
a scalar tuning parameter that skews the local step-selection rule toward preferred neighbors
the per-step transition probability governing how a mobile token hops between adjacent elements
the substrate graph of interacting elements over which the process unfolds, with an arbitrary connectivity distribution
the steady-state probability of finding the mobile token at a given element
the mean number of steps for the token to travel between two designated elements
a critical value of the injection rate above which the system's collective behavior qualitatively changes
the rate at which discrete units are injected into the system
a local connectivity attribute of an element in the substrate that biases local transitions
a system of discrete elements joined by pairwise connections
an assignment partitioning elements into cohesive classes
a scalar aggregate quantifying the quality of a partition by comparing observed to expected connection density
recurring higher-order connection sub-patterns among small groups of elements
statistical correlations between elements that extend beyond immediate neighbours
the local density of connections within a class
the internal state carried by each elementary unit of the system
the network structure specifying which elementary units interact with which
the local interaction that makes one unit's state influence a neighboring unit's state
a collective aggregate that emerges macroscopically from many local interactions
an empirically measured quantity used to test the model's collective predictions
a scalar rate parameter tuning how fast the process propagates through the system
a critical value of the control parameter below which the process cannot propagate at all
a collective aggregate — the eventual fraction of units ever reached — that becomes non-zero once the control parameter exceeds the critical point
the connectivity structure linking units, whose topology ranges from homogeneous to strongly heterogeneous
correlations between the connectivity degrees of adjacent units in the interaction structure
the activation state of an individual unit
the initial growth rate at which the propagating quantity spreads early on
the overall size of the system
a higher-order relational network that joins three distinct classes of nodes through multi-endpoint links
a scalar size parameter whose large-value limit renders aggregate quantities exactly computable
a behavioral pattern in which a single node is linked to many counterparts, inducing correlations across the network
analytically derived aggregate structural properties of the idealized model
empirically measured aggregate structural properties of the real system
the discrepancy between the derived and the measured structural properties
a class of active nodes that generate the higher-order links
a class of passive nodes that receive the links
a class of annotation nodes attached to the passive nodes
a set of discrete units joined by pairwise links whose connectivity structure is modeled
a scalar tuning the propensity for two units adjacent to a common unit to also be directly linked
a scalar setting the overall density of pairwise links in the system
the fraction of the system contained in a single macroscopic connected piece, becoming non-zero past a critical point
the critical parameter value at which the macroscopic connected piece first emerges
the fraction of elements retained under a stochastic pruning process applied to the system
the critical value of the retained fraction at which a system-spanning connected cluster first appears
the distribution over sizes of the disconnected finite pieces of the system
the fraction of the system belonging to a spanning cluster under the stochastic pruning process, non-zero past a critical point
a set of interacting units joined by pairwise links, coupled to a second such set whose functioning is mutually required
a mutual-dependency coupling that ties units across the two sub-systems so that survival of one requires the other
a scalar tuning parameter setting the mean number of links per unit
a collective aggregate (fraction of units in the largest surviving connected cluster) that stays finite above a critical point and vanishes to total collapse below it
a critical value of the control parameter separating a functioning phase from complete fragmentation of both sub-systems
an externally imposed perturbation that removes a small fraction of units to initiate the process
a self-amplifying feedback process in which failures propagate back and forth between the coupled sub-systems, escalating each round
a parameter describing the breadth/heterogeneity of the per-unit link-count distribution
a measured robustness of the coupled system to random removal of units
a network of directed coupling links along which signals flow between discrete units
a second, distinct network of symmetric coupling links joining the same set of units
an externally imposed stimulus applied to a selected subset of units
the activation state of each unit as it propagates through the coupled network
statistical and topological descriptors of connectivity (link-count distribution, multiplicity, small-world index, recurring subgraph frequencies)
units occupying central hub positions that mediate a disproportionate share of signal routing
collective activation patterns that emerge across the network in response to input
a locally self-reinforcing dynamical field defined on each discrete unit
a second dynamical field that antagonizes and suppresses the self-reinforcing one
a large random network of links coupling discrete units together
transport of the two fields between linked units across the network
a scalar ratio of the two fields' transport rates that tunes the onset of instability
a critical value at which the spatially uniform state loses stability
a collective aggregate measuring splitting of units into high- and low- groups, nonzero past the critical point
the number of coupling links attached to a unit (its connectivity)
coexistence of multiple stationary states with history-dependent (hysteretic) selection
a large collection of individual units, each carrying a time-evolving directional state
a local interaction that tends to align the directional states of neighboring units
the proximity-based network specifying which units mutually influence each other
a collective aggregate that becomes non-zero once the units coordinate, distinguishing the few macroscopic states
a scalar tuning parameter whose variation drives the system between distinct macroscopic states
a critical value at which the collective state changes qualitatively, marked by a discontinuity
a measurable response quantity that diverges algebraically near the critical point
a scalar fraction that tunes the system and drives a qualitative change in its collective behavior
a critical value of the tuning parameter above which the system's behavior changes qualitatively
a collective aggregate describing the population-wide adopted state that can be flipped from one dominant value to another
a measurable timescale for the whole system to reach a single uniform collective state
a fixed subpopulation that persistently biases other units toward one state and is itself immune to influence
an exponential growth-rate coefficient governing how the relaxation timescale scales with system size
the topology of interactions connecting the individual units of the system
the discrete state carried by an individual unit that can switch under local influence
pairwise coupling strength between two constituent units of the system, estimated from their co-fluctuation
a scalar tuning parameter applied to pairwise couplings that admits a link only when the coupling exceeds it
a network whose nodes are the units and whose edges connect pairs whose coupling exceeds the tuning parameter
the collective grouping of units into connected clusters that emerges from the network structure
a scalar measure characterizing the scaling heterogeneity of a unit's fluctuation series
an external regime shift that perturbs the whole system over a distinguished time interval
a scalar tuning parameter in [0,1] setting the fraction of components deactivated on each presented instance
a stochastic process that randomly deactivates a subset of components independently for each presented instance
an individual component that adapts to respond to a recurring pattern in the inputs
mutual dependence between components whereby one component is only useful in the specific context of certain other components
the large ensemble of interconnected components that jointly process each instance
the ratio of ensemble capacity to the number of available example instances
performance measured on unseen, held-out instances not used during adaptation
the failure mode of fitting the available sample closely while performing poorly on unseen instances
a simple local rule that generates new connections between two elements that already share a common neighbor
a mesoscopic collective aggregate: densely-linked subgroups that emerge spontaneously from the dynamics
the relational substrate of elements and pairwise links on which the growth process operates
a scalar tuning parameter measuring how densely populated the substrate is with links
a scalar tuning parameter controlling the degree of stochasticity in the attachment procedure
a per-element intrinsic attractivity that biases which elements accrue new connections
a scalar tuning parameter measuring the spread/heterogeneity of the attractivity across elements
an alternative collective organization in which a few dominant elements are shared among many groups
measurable structural statistics of the substrate, including heavy-tailed connectivity counts and high local transitivity
a raw single-channel time-series input signal fed into the system
a local, translation-invariant mapping that transforms the input into intermediate representations
position-invariant intermediate representations extracted from the input
a bidirectional sequential mapping that couples each element to its neighbours in an ordered sequence
learned rules governing the succession between ordered discrete categories
the discrete category assigned to each successive element of the sequence
a scalar aggregate score measuring agreement of predicted categories with a reference standard
a staged optimization procedure that fits the parameters of the mappings
a low-dimensional set of tuning parameters that indexes candidate high-dimensional configurations
a trained transformation mapping the low-dimensional parameters onto high-dimensional structured configurations
an externally supplied exemplar encoding the target statistical structure to be reproduced
a high-dimensional generated configuration realized from the parameters
measured quantities predicted from a configuration through a forward operator
the distribution over configurations conditioned on the measured quantities — the collective target the sampler converges toward
an iterative stochastic sampling process that explores the conditioned target distribution
externally imposed pointwise constraints incorporated during inference
a scalar tuning parameter in a bounded range, the fraction of units adhering to an imposed behavioral restriction
a collective aggregate measuring the extent of propagation across the population; sustained in one regime and suppressed in another
a narrow critical band of the control parameter (a tipping range) separating an uncontrolled regime from a controlled one
a fine-grained network of individually resolved interacting units over which the process propagates
a per-unit status variable whose activation probability depends on a unit attribute and differs by unit class
an externally applied intervention that detects and removes or isolates active units
an externally applied intervention restricting cross-boundary inflow of new active units
a secondary intervention whose effect is contingent on being combined with a high value of the control parameter
the elapsed time required to drive the order parameter down to a controlled level
a per-unit quantity held by each member of a population that evolves in time
the strength/rate of pairwise transfer of the quantity between units
a random multiplicative fluctuation acting on each unit's quantity independently
a collective aggregate measuring concentration of the quantity onto a few units, non-zero in one phase
the population-wide distribution of the per-unit quantity, characterized by heavy power-law tails
a scalar tuning parameter setting the balance between pairwise transfer and random fluctuation
the connectivity/topology and range over which transfers between units may occur
an externally imposed uniform redistributive drain applied across the population
a tunable input distribution over per-element local connectivity that governs the system's structure
a macroscopic connected aggregate whose relative size is zero below a critical point and becomes non-zero above it
a critical value of the control parameter at which the collective aggregate first emerges
an ensemble of elements joined by pairwise links, drawn at random subject to the input distribution
the number of links attached to an individual element
the average size of the finite connected clusters an element belongs to
the average number of steps separating two elements along links, and the count of elements a fixed distance away
a network whose connectivity distribution is strongly heterogeneous (lacking a characteristic scale), serving as the substrate over which a dynamical process propagates between coupled units
a scalar tuning parameter setting the per-contact rate at which the active condition is transmitted from one unit to a neighbor
a collective steady-state aggregate giving the fraction of units in the active condition; it vanishes below a critical point and becomes non-zero above it
a critical value of the control parameter separating an inactive phase from an active phase; found to collapse to zero on the heterogeneous substrate
the two-valued condition of an individual unit, toggling between an active and an inactive state
the average persistence time of the active configuration across the population
a scalar tuning parameter whose value sets the macroscopic regime of the system
a critical value of the tuning parameter at which the collective state changes qualitatively
a collective aggregate that is zero on one side of the threshold and becomes non-zero on the other
a pairwise interaction strength that aligns neighboring units
the network of connections over which units interact
a distribution-valued parameter governing how many connections each unit has
a response function measuring how strongly the collective aggregate reacts to an external influence
a response function measuring how the system's energy content changes with the tuning parameter
a higher-order statistical moment of the connectivity distribution whose divergence drives anomalous behavior
a network of pairwise connections among units over which an activated state propagates
a per-unit discrete state that advances irreversibly through a fixed sequence of stages (available, activated, then spent)
a scalar tunable probability that an interaction along a connection succeeds in propagating the activated state
the timescale over which an activated unit remains able to propagate to its neighbours
a collective aggregate measuring the total extent of propagation, becoming macroscopic past a critical point
the critical value of the control parameter above which macroscopic propagation emerges
the per-connection coupling strength governing propagation between adjacent units, allowed to be non-uniform and correlated across connections
a heterogeneous connectivity structure linking discrete units, over which a propagation process unfolds
fraction of units in a receptive state, able to be activated upon contact with an active unit
fraction of units in an active state that transmit activation to their neighbors
fraction of units in a terminal inert state that no longer transmit or receive activation
per-contact rate at which an active unit converts a receptive neighbor into an active unit
rate at which an active unit transitions into the terminal inert state after contacting a non-receptive unit
the final aggregate fraction of units ever activated, a collective outcome emerging from the dynamics
a structural parameter quantifying the degree of connectivity heterogeneity across the network
the total number of units, invariant under the dynamics so that the state fractions always sum to a constant
microscopic units whose local assignments evolve under mutual influence
a pairwise coupling acting between two units
the network of pairwise couplings connecting the units
emergent collective clusters that self-organize from the interacting units
a rank-magnitude distribution of cluster sizes following a scale-free power law
a set of directed links assigned between elementary units, either by a fixed rule or built up incrementally
the abundance level of each unit, evolving under coupled dynamical equations
the pairwise interaction strengths between units that mediate their mutual dynamics
a collective persistence property that is non-zero when the assembled system endures rather than collapsing
the total count of units comprising the system
the rate at which new units are introduced into the system, either by insertion or by variation of existing units
